title: AI companies hire forward-deployed engineers to bridge code and ...
full_text:
The FT reports that AI companies are discovering what we've known since day one: you can't scale AI adoption from a distance.
The FT reports that AI companies are discovering what we've known since day one: you can't scale AI adoption from a distance.
Apparently, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Cohere are now hiring "forward-deployed engineers" - developers who can both code and talk to customers. Job postings are claimed to be up 800% between January and September this year.
From my perspective, this isn't a hiring trend, it's the only way to deliver AI. Because the people who can only talk about AI don't really know what's possible, and the people who can only code it can't figure out the problem to solve.
The ability to code and communicate are two halves of the coin. We need engineers who can talk to clients, figure out vague problems (because at the start, they always are) and apply creativity to find a great solution.
It's no coincidence that we've had mention of FDEs on our Barnacle Labs home page for some time now.
https://lnkd.in/eeCnr479
The new hot job in AI: forward-deployed engineers
